seo.title: Polymarket USDC Withdrawal Guide | Fees & Speeds
seo.description: Learn how to bridge your USDC off Polygon from Polymarket quickly and cheaply to centralized exchanges.

tax_reporting.notes: Polymarket is a DeFi protocol and does not issue tax forms. Users must self-report crypto gains/losses per IRS guidance on digital assets.

withdrawal_methods.pro_tip: Ensure your receiving exchange (like Coinbase or Kraken) supports USDC deposits directly on the Polygon network. Do NOT send to an Ethereum ERC-20 address or funds will be permanently lost.
For massive withdrawals (over $50,000), break it into smaller transactions to minimize slippage in the Uniswap bridging pool.

verification.kyc_trigger: Only required if using 3rd party fiat on-ramps like MoonPay
verification.pro_tip: Polymarket relies on Web3 proxy wallets. You control your keys, meaning no central authority asks for your ID.

withdrawal_experience.rollover_rules: None. Funds are always liquid unless locked in open market positions.
withdrawal_experience.cancellation_window: Crypto transactions cannot be reversed once broadcast to the network.

faqs.question: How do I withdraw from Polymarket?
faqs.answer: You withdraw USDC directly to your Web3 wallet, or to a centralized exchange that supports Polygon network deposits.

faqs.question: What are Polymarket withdrawal fees?
faqs.answer: Polymarket does not charge withdrawal fees. You only pay standard Polygon network gas fees, which are typically fractions of a cent.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw to my bank account from Polymarket?
faqs.answer: No, you must withdraw USDC to a crypto exchange (like Coinbase) first, sell the USDC for USD, and then initiate an ACH transfer to your bank.
